point_estimates.note: Minor numerical differences between R and Python arise from different linear algebra backends and optimizer defaults

standard_errors.note: SE differences between R and Python are common due to different HC/cluster implementations and degrees-of-freedom corrections

p_values.note: P-values near significance boundaries (0.01, 0.05, 0.10) should be flagged regardless of tolerance

sample_sizes.note: Any discrepancy indicates a data handling difference that must be resolved

common_divergence_sources: Floating-point handling in fixed effects absorption
NA/NaN handling defaults (na.rm in R vs dropna in pandas)
